description: Concise synthetic and heavily paraphrased role-pattern examples for offline classifier and tailoring calibration.

examples.title: RTL Design Engineer
examples.description: Responsibilities include implementing SystemVerilog RTL blocks, reviewing microarchitecture specifications, debugging simulation failures, and supporting synthesis handoff. Qualifications include digital logic, Verilog, Python scripting, timing awareness, and architecture fundamentals.
examples.notes: Pure RTL implementation role should use the Digital IC master unchanged.

examples.title: ASIC Front-End Design Engineer
examples.description: Own front-end ASIC design for packet-processing logic, including RTL coding, lint, CDC cleanup, synthesis constraints, and block-level timing closure. The role emphasizes SystemVerilog, low-power design, and handoff to physical design.
examples.notes: ASIC front-end design is a clear Digital IC label.

examples.title: CPU Microarchitecture RTL Engineer
examples.description: Design pipeline control, branch recovery, exception flow, cache interface logic, and performance counters for an in-house CPU core. Use SystemVerilog, simulation debug, assertions, and architecture modelling to close design features.
examples.notes: CPU RTL ownership should not drift to verification.

examples.title: GPU Datapath Design Engineer
examples.description: Develop arithmetic datapaths and scheduler RTL for graphics and compute workloads. Responsibilities include SystemVerilog implementation, synthesis experiments, area and timing tradeoffs, and simulation-based performance analysis.
examples.notes: GPU datapath design is Digital IC.

examples.title: NPU RTL Design Engineer
examples.description: Build accelerator RTL for matrix multiply and tensor data movement. The work covers scratchpad memories, DMA engines, fixed-point datapaths, synthesis constraints, and cycle-level performance modelling in Python.
examples.notes: RTL base with ML accelerator evidence can justify SparrowML crossover.

examples.title: FPGA RTL Engineer
examples.description: Implement streaming datapaths, AXI interfaces, and timing-clean RTL for FPGA prototypes. Responsibilities include Verilog, SystemVerilog, Vivado builds, simulation debug, and hardware bring-up support.
examples.notes: FPGA-heavy RTL role remains Digital IC.

examples.title: Cache Coherence RTL Engineer
examples.description: Own coherent interconnect and cache-controller RTL for multicore SoCs. Work includes MSI/MESI protocol design, writeback handling, arbitration, assertions, and performance counter analysis.
examples.notes: Coherence/interconnect maps to existing Digital IC base projects.

examples.title: Physical-Aware RTL Designer
examples.description: Create timing-aware RTL and collaborate with implementation teams on synthesis, constraints, and floorplan feedback. Must understand clocking, reset strategy, DFT handoff, and SystemVerilog coding style.

examples.title: Embedded Processor RTL Engineer
examples.description: Design RISC-V subsystem blocks, bus bridges, interrupt logic, and tightly coupled memories. Use SystemVerilog, C tests, Python models, and waveform debug to validate hardware behavior.
examples.notes: C/Python are support tools; RTL ownership dominates.

examples.title: SoC Fabric Design Engineer
examples.description: Develop NoC adapters, AXI protocol bridges, arbitration policies, and clock-domain crossing wrappers for a configurable SoC fabric. Role requires RTL design, timing analysis, and microarchitecture documentation.
examples.notes: Interconnect design should not be classified as software.

examples.title: Accelerator Architecture RTL Engineer
examples.description: Evaluate ML accelerator dataflows with Python performance models, then implement selected tensor datapaths and memory scheduling in SystemVerilog RTL. Qualifications include quantized inference awareness and ASIC design experience.

examples.title: RTL Synthesis Methodology Engineer
examples.description: Improve front-end synthesis quality for RTL blocks, triage timing regressions, maintain lint and constraint flows, and guide designers on microarchitecture changes for area and timing.

examples.title: Senior ASIC RTL Engineer
examples.description: Lead RTL implementation of a data-movement subsystem with DMA, SRAM controllers, and bus fabric integration. Work includes SystemVerilog, synthesis, STA feedback, and design reviews.

examples.title: FPGA Acceleration RTL Developer
examples.description: Create FPGA RTL accelerators for streaming analytics, optimize AXI data movement, close timing in vendor tools, and debug hardware traces from prototype boards.

examples.title: ML Accelerator RTL Architect
examples.description: Define and implement tensor accelerator microarchitecture, including sparse execution, INT8 datapaths, memory tiling, and cycle-level workload analysis for inference models.

examples.title: UVM IP Verification Engineer
examples.description: Build reusable UVM agents, sequences, scoreboards, coverage models, and assertions for a configurable IP block. Responsibilities include constrained-random testing, regression triage, and protocol debug.

examples.title: SoC Verification Engineer
examples.description: Own subsystem verification planning, UVM environments, monitors, checkers, coverage closure, and simulation regressions for SoC integration. Python scripting is used for automation and triage.

examples.title: CPU Verification Engineer
examples.description: Verify CPU pipeline behavior with random instruction streams, reference-model comparison, assertions, functional coverage, and debug of exceptions, stalls, redirects, and memory ordering.
examples.notes: CPU verification can emphasize Sparrow-V verification block.

examples.title: GPU Verification Engineer
examples.description: Develop verification plans and UVM tests for GPU command processing and datapath units. Responsibilities include scoreboards, coverage, debug automation, and multi-seed regression closure.
examples.notes: GPU context should not switch to Digital IC design.

examples.title: Formal Verification Engineer
examples.description: Write SystemVerilog assertions, prove protocol properties, create abstraction models, debug counterexamples, and integrate formal results with simulation coverage for RTL signoff.

examples.title: Pre-Silicon Validation Engineer
examples.description: Create validation tests for a pre-silicon SoC platform using simulation, emulation, protocol checks, coverage metrics, and Python automation for failing-seed minimization.

examples.title: Verification Infrastructure Engineer
examples.description: Build Python tooling for regression launch, log parsing, coverage dashboards, seed scheduling, and reproducible bug triage for UVM simulation farms.
examples.notes: Verification infrastructure is hybrid but should keep verification base.

examples.title: AXI Protocol Verification Engineer
examples.description: Verify AXI and AXI-Stream blocks with UVM agents, BFMs, protocol assertions, coverage, malformed transaction tests, backpressure scenarios, and scoreboard-based checking.
examples.notes: Aligns with AXI router verification block.

examples.title: SoC Security Verification Engineer
examples.description: Develop tests and assertions for privilege checks, MMIO access control, secure state transitions, and protocol misuse. Work includes clean-versus-bug simulation oracles and evidence-backed reports.
examples.notes: Security context maps to verification block.

examples.title: Coverage Closure Engineer
examples.description: Analyze functional coverage gaps, write targeted constrained-random sequences, debug scoreboard mismatches, and drive weekly regression closure for RTL blocks.

examples.title: RISC-V DV Engineer
examples.description: Build differential testing and random verification for RISC-V cores, including instruction generators, reference-model comparison, trace checking, assertions, and focused regressions.
examples.notes: Sparrow-V verification is the strongest compatible block.

examples.title: Python Automation Engineer for UVM
examples.description: Develop Python services that orchestrate UVM regressions, collect waveforms and logs, rank failures, and report coverage trends for hardware verification teams.
examples.notes: Hybrid verification/software but UVM context should be visible.

examples.title: IP Verification Engineer
examples.description: Create verification plans, UVM components, assertions, reference models, coverage points, and random sequences for a configurable digital IP.

examples.title: Formal and Simulation Verification Engineer
examples.description: Combine assertion-based formal proofs with simulation tests, protocol checkers, coverage closure, and counterexample debug for SoC control logic.

examples.title: Hardware Regression Infrastructure Engineer
examples.description: Maintain Python tooling for simulation farms, failure clustering, coverage aggregation, and triage workflows used by UVM and pre-silicon validation teams.

examples.title: Backend Software Engineer
examples.description: Build backend APIs, data models, background workers, observability, and service integrations for a customer-facing platform. Requirements include Python, SQL, HTTP, Docker, and distributed systems fundamentals.

examples.title: Full Stack Engineer
examples.description: Develop React interfaces, backend APIs, authentication flows, database migrations, and asynchronous job processing. The role values product sense, testing, and operational reliability.

examples.title: Developer Tools Engineer
examples.description: Create CLI tools, local services, CI integrations, test runners, and documentation automation for engineering teams. Work includes Python, TypeScript, GitHub workflows, and packaging.

examples.title: Data Infrastructure Engineer
examples.description: Design ingestion services, SQL schemas, batch processing, monitoring, and data quality checks for analytics pipelines. Requirements include Python, distributed systems, cloud storage, and workflow orchestration.

examples.title: Performance Engineering Software Developer
examples.description: Profile service latency, optimize memory use, build benchmarking harnesses, analyze production traces, and improve runtime efficiency for backend systems.

examples.title: Systems Software Engineer
examples.description: Work on Linux services, concurrency, networking, storage interfaces, and low-level performance in C++ and Python. The role does not involve RTL or hardware verification.
examples.notes: Systems software can mention low-level concepts without being RTL.

examples.title: Platform API Engineer
examples.description: Own internal APIs, service contracts, queue workers, database access patterns, deployment automation, and reliability improvements for a multi-tenant platform.

examples.title: Backend Engineer for AI Product
examples.description: Build APIs, job queues, prompt-storage services, evaluation dashboards, and billing-safe execution paths around existing AI models. The role integrates models but does not train them.

examples.title: Security Tooling Engineer
examples.description: Develop services that collect vulnerability signals, deduplicate findings, manage triage workflows, and expose APIs for engineering teams. Requirements include Python, SQL, and secure software practices.

examples.title: Build and Release Engineer
examples.description: Maintain CI/CD pipelines, package builds, release automation, test reporting, artifact storage, and developer productivity tooling across Python and JavaScript projects.

examples.title: Observability Platform Engineer
examples.description: Create logging, metrics, tracing, alerting, and incident-analysis systems for production services. Work includes backend APIs, storage, dashboards, and reliability engineering.

examples.title: Edge Runtime Software Engineer
examples.description: Develop C++ runtime components, device integration layers, telemetry, update mechanisms, and performance tests for edge applications. No RTL design or ML model training is required.

examples.title: Senior Backend Engineer
examples.description: Design scalable APIs, database schemas, async workers, auth flows, and operational dashboards for a local-first productivity product.

examples.title: Engineering Productivity Developer
examples.description: Build internal tools for test selection, code review automation, artifact validation, and local developer workflows using Python and TypeScript.

examples.title: AI Platform Backend Engineer
examples.description: Implement APIs, evaluation queues, usage tracking, database-backed experiment metadata, and integration tests for an ML-enabled product surface.

examples.title: Applied Machine Learning Engineer
examples.description: Train, evaluate, and deploy models for user-facing predictions. Work includes feature pipelines, PyTorch training, offline metrics, error analysis, and inference-quality monitoring.

examples.title: Machine Learning Engineer
examples.description: Own model training, feature extraction, experiment tracking, model evaluation, batch inference, and deployment support for production ML systems.

examples.title: Speech and NLP ML Engineer
examples.description: Develop speech and text models, evaluate language and acoustic features, tune thresholds, analyze errors, and prepare reproducible training and inference workflows.
examples.notes: Speech/NLP aligns with dementia speech project.

examples.title: Computer Vision ML Engineer
examples.description: Train and optimize vision models, prepare datasets, evaluate accuracy and latency, quantize models for edge deployment, and monitor inference behavior.

examples.title: Edge Inference Engineer
examples.description: Optimize ML inference on constrained devices using quantization, runtime profiling, tensor serialization, C deployment paths, and accuracy-latency tradeoff analysis.
examples.notes: Edge inference can emphasize SparrowML within ML.

examples.title: Model Quantization Engineer
examples.description: Develop quantization and pruning workflows, measure accuracy drops, benchmark INT8 inference, and integrate optimized models into deployment runtimes.

examples.title: ML Systems Engineer
examples.description: Build training and inference infrastructure, model packaging, benchmark harnesses, GPU utilization dashboards, and reproducible deployment workflows.
examples.notes: ML systems can be hybrid but model context dominates.

examples.title: LLM Inference Engineer
examples.description: Optimize model-serving latency, batching, quantization, cache behavior, prompt evaluation, and reliability for large language model inference services.

examples.title: Recommendation ML Engineer
examples.description: Train ranking models, evaluate offline metrics, run feature ablations, monitor inference quality, and collaborate with platform teams on deployment.

examples.title: ML Evaluation Engineer
examples.description: Design evaluation datasets, metrics, error-analysis tools, threshold calibration, blind evaluation workflows, and reporting for model quality improvements.
examples.notes: Evaluation is ML when model metrics are central.

examples.title: Production Data Scientist
examples.description: Build predictive models, validate features, run experiments, compare baselines, and hand off reproducible model artifacts for production inference.

examples.title: Model Optimization Engineer
examples.description: Optimize trained neural networks for latency, memory, power, and accuracy across TensorFlow Lite, PyTorch export, quantization, and edge inference pipelines.

examples.title: Speech ML Engineer
examples.description: Create speech classification models, combine acoustic and text features, evaluate F1 and AUROC, and build reproducible blind-test workflows.

examples.title: Quantized Edge ML Engineer
examples.description: Train and quantize models for constrained devices, generate runtime artifacts, compare accuracy and latency, and benchmark inference on edge hardware.

examples.title: LLM Model Serving Engineer
examples.description: Improve inference throughput, batching, model loading, quantization, evaluation, and online quality monitoring for large language model services.

examples.title: NPU Compiler Engineer
examples.description: Develop compiler passes, tensor lowering, quantization-aware scheduling, and runtime code generation for a neural processing unit. Work touches ML models, C runtime, and hardware execution constraints.
examples.notes: NPU compiler can reasonably land ML or software; automatic tailoring should stay bounded.

examples.title: ML Accelerator Architect
examples.description: Explore tensor workloads, sparse execution, quantized inference, memory hierarchy, and RTL feasibility for accelerator blocks. Responsibilities include modelling and microarchitecture proposals.

examples.title: Verification Tooling Software Engineer
examples.description: Build Python and web tooling for UVM regression management, coverage triage, waveform metadata, failing-test clustering, and hardware verification dashboards.
examples.notes: Hybrid but no approved cross-family software insertion for verification.

examples.title: GPU Performance Engineer
examples.description: Analyze GPU workloads, build benchmark harnesses, profile kernels, inspect memory behavior, and recommend software or architecture changes for compute throughput.
examples.notes: Ambiguous GPU performance role should be reviewable.

examples.title: Embedded AI Engineer
examples.description: Integrate quantized models into embedded C++ applications, profile latency and memory, manage sensor pipelines, and coordinate with ML teams on accuracy constraints.
examples.notes: Embedded AI integration is software/ML hybrid.

examples.title: Inference Runtime Engineer
examples.description: Build C++ runtime kernels, tensor memory planners, batching logic, and model loading paths for optimized inference across CPU and accelerator targets.

examples.title: Backend Engineer for Model Evaluation Platform
examples.description: Implement APIs, queues, dataset metadata, result storage, and dashboards for ML evaluation workflows. Model training is handled by separate teams.
examples.notes: AI-product platform should not automatically swap to ML project.

examples.title: FPGA Acceleration Engineer
examples.description: Map compute kernels to FPGA RTL, optimize streaming dataflow, integrate host software, close timing, and benchmark application-level throughput.

examples.title: AI Hardware Applications Engineer
examples.description: Help customers map neural networks to accelerator hardware, debug model accuracy, explain performance counters, and coordinate with compiler and RTL teams.
examples.notes: Applications role is intentionally ambiguous and reviewable.

examples.title: ML Tooling Engineer for Silicon Teams
examples.description: Create Python tools that compare model traces, quantized tensors, RTL simulation outputs, and hardware performance counters for accelerator validation.
examples.notes: Ambiguous tooling crossing ML and verification should require review.

examples.title: RTL Security Automation Engineer
examples.description: Develop generated tests, simulation harnesses, MMIO trace tools, minimizers, and reports for security bug discovery in small SoC RTL designs.

examples.title: Model Serving Backend Engineer
examples.description: Build high-throughput APIs, request batching, model version routing, cache-aware inference scheduling, metrics, and rollback controls for deployed models.

examples.title: Hardware Software Codesign Engineer
examples.description: Evaluate workloads, write C/Python models, propose ISA extensions, prototype RTL datapaths, and benchmark software kernels against accelerator behavior.
examples.notes: Hardware/software co-design with Digital IC base.

examples.title: Verification Data Platform Engineer
examples.description: Build databases, APIs, and dashboards for simulation results, coverage history, failure clustering, and regression-health reporting for chip verification teams.

examples.title: Edge ML Compiler Runtime Engineer
examples.description: Lower PyTorch models to compact tensor formats, generate C runtime code, schedule sparse vector kernels, and benchmark execution on small processors.

examples.title: Neural Accelerator Toolchain Engineer
examples.description: Develop compiler and runtime tooling for neural accelerator targets, including quantized tensor packing, workload generation, and hardware-aware benchmarking.

examples.title: GPU Kernel Performance Engineer
examples.description: Profile kernels, memory traffic, occupancy, batching, and service integration for model inference workloads running on GPUs.
examples.notes: Held-out GPU performance for ML inference.

examples.title: Simulation Infrastructure Software Engineer
examples.description: Build tools for launching RTL simulations, collecting UVM logs, parsing coverage, deduplicating failures, and tracking verification regressions.

examples.title: FPGA AI Acceleration Engineer
examples.description: Prototype quantized inference kernels on FPGA, integrate host software, close timing, and compare model accuracy and throughput across hardware builds.

examples.title: AI Hardware Customer Engineer
examples.description: Debug customer model deployments on accelerator platforms, explain performance counters, triage runtime issues, and coordinate fixes across compiler and hardware teams.
examples.notes: Held-out ambiguous applications role should be reviewable.

examples.title: Analog IC Design Engineer
examples.description: Design amplifiers, data converters, PLLs, bias circuits, and transistor-level schematics. Responsibilities include SPICE simulations, layout review, and silicon characterization.
examples.notes: Analog IC is outside supported digital families.

examples.title: RF Circuit Designer
examples.description: Work on RF front-end circuits, mixers, LNAs, impedance matching, electromagnetic simulation, and lab characterization for wireless products.

examples.title: Mechanical Design Engineer
examples.description: Design enclosures, thermal fixtures, mechanical assemblies, tolerance stacks, and manufacturing drawings using CAD and vendor coordination.

examples.title: Technical Product Manager
examples.description: Own product roadmap, customer interviews, launch planning, requirement prioritization, stakeholder communication, and business metrics for a developer platform.

examples.title: Enterprise Account Executive
examples.description: Manage sales pipeline, customer relationships, renewal forecasts, executive presentations, and commercial negotiations for technical products.
examples.notes: Sales role is out of scope.

examples.title: Bioinformatics Scientist
examples.description: Analyze genomic datasets, build statistical workflows, interpret biological signals, and collaborate with wet-lab teams on experimental design.

examples.title: Quantitative Finance Analyst
examples.description: Develop trading signals, portfolio risk models, market data analysis, and research reports for investment strategies.
examples.notes: Finance role is out of supported scope.

examples.title: Analog Layout Engineer
examples.description: Create custom analog layouts, match devices, handle parasitic extraction, review DRC and LVS, and collaborate on transistor-level circuit constraints.

examples.title: Engineering Program Manager
examples.description: Coordinate schedules, risks, cross-team milestones, status reporting, and launch readiness for engineering programs without owning implementation.

examples.title: Field Applications Sales Manager
examples.description: Manage customer accounts, coordinate demos, gather commercial requirements, forecast revenue, and support sales strategy for semiconductor products.
examples.notes: Held-out business role with semiconductor context.
